summary: Iteration 5 - Tested NEW editable Step 3 breakdown + saved-quote detail modal + multi-save fix + business profile editing. Backend pytest: 19/19 PASS (9 auth + 5 quotes + 5 new profile/multi-save). Frontend Playwright e2e on https://pro-quote-builder-1.preview.emergentagent.com (demo@terrainpro.com): login -> trial banner '7 Days Remaining' OK; estimator full flow (Step1 customer + job-chip-turf_irrigation -> Step2 turf_area_sqm=60 -> Generate) produced wiz-live-totals in ~25s; Step 3 rendered TWO tiers ('Material Costs' + 'Labour & Earthmoving') with 6 editable QTY/RATE/linetotal rows; QTY edit (3 -> 999) instantly recalculated wiz-linetotal-turf_irrigation-1 ($225 -> $74,925), total-subtotal ($3,150 -> $77,850) and total-final ($4,158 -> $102,762); RATE edit also recalculated linetotal; markup change to 25% matched the spec math exactly (subtotal*0.25 markup, (sub+markup)*0.10 GST, sub+markup+gst final - within $1 tolerance); wiz-save-quote -> 'Draft Saved' label + dashboard row appeared; wiz-reset + new flow + second wiz-save-quote also transitioned to 'Draft Saved' (multi-save bug fixed); quote-detail-modal opens from quote-row-0 and renders Scope + Assumptions + Export PDF + Close; business-meta-edit-btn -> business-name-input + business-abn-input -> business-meta-save persists and DOM shows TEST_ACME EARTHWORKS + ABN 11122233344. Two minor UX gaps and one click-interception quirk noted below (non-blocking).

ui_bugs.issue: Playwright `page.click('[data-testid=quote-row-0]', force=True)` does NOT open the quote-detail-modal; only a programmatic JS `.click()` triggers it. Real users using mouse work fine, but this suggests the onClick handler is attached to an inner element that gets intercepted by a sibling/overlay (likely the table-row wrapper vs. a child cell). Worth verifying the click target is the row itself, not blocked by status-pill / dot overlays.

Clicking 'Start a new quote' (wiz-reset) does NOT clear Step 1 inputs: customer-name still shows previous client and job-chip selection count remained '1 selected' (chip still selected). Users have to manually overwrite. Save itself works on the new quote (no functional blocker), but a fresh reset should clear all wizard state.

Observed during one rerun: only the hardcoded sample row (Melinda Rankine / $12,400-$14,500 / June 5) was visible right after saving a quote and navigating to /dashboard, even though backend GET /api/quotes returns the saved items. Likely the fallback sample row from iteration_4's code-review note ('Dashboard.jsx falls back to a hardcoded sample row when GET /api/quotes returns []') being shown due to a transient empty/loading response. Backend persistence itself is fine (verified by pytest).

action_items: wiz-reset should fully clear wizard state (customer fields, job-chip selection, measurements) so the next quote starts fresh.
Verify quote-row-0 click target / z-index so a normal user click reliably opens quote-detail-modal (current Playwright pointer click is intercepted; only JS .click() works).
Remove or feature-flag the Dashboard hardcoded sample-quote fallback before production so empty-state doesn't masquerade as real data.
critical_code_review_comments: EstimatorWizard.jsx handleReset (wiz-reset) clears `quote`/`saved` but appears to leave Step 1 customer fields (wiz-customer-name/street/suburb) and selected job chips intact. Either reset them to defaults or rename the button to 'New quote from this customer'.

Dashboard.jsx still renders a hardcoded sample row when GET /api/quotes is empty or slow to load. This makes empty-state ambiguous and was observed leaking into the post-save view during a fast goto. Move sample data behind a `?demo=1` flag or remove.

Saved-quote click target: <tr data-testid='quote-row-N'> with onClick={setSelected} - Playwright .click(force=True) failed to open the modal even though innerText was correct; the onClick may be on a non-pointer element or wrapped by a sibling with pointer-events:auto. Consider promoting it to a real <button> or adding role='button' + tabIndex.
Server.py /api/auth/profile PUT correctly handles partial updates (verified). No issues.
Server.py /api/quotes POST correctly inserts a new doc per call (multi-save unblocked, verified backend+frontend). No issues.

Backend code-review carries forward from iteration_4: CORS allow_origins='*' + allow_credentials=True; no pagination on /api/quotes (50-cap); SaveQuoteRequest accepts raw dicts without nested validation - all non-blocking.

Step 3 math is exact: confirmed via Playwright that markup=subtotal*pct, GST=(subtotal+markup)*0.10, total=subtotal+markup+GST within $1 rounding (25% markup: $7,920 subtotal -> $1,980 markup / $990 GST / $10,890 final).

success_rate.frontend: 95% — all 11 functional checks (login, trial banner, full estimator, editable Step3, inline QTY/RATE recalc, markup math, save A, reset+save B multi-save, detail modal open/close, business profile edit) PASS. 3 minor non-blocking UX notes captured under ui_bugs.

test_credentials: demo@terrainpro.com / trade1234. Plus QA-created users via /api/auth/register (qa_<uuid>@test.com / secret123).
seed_data_creation: pytest run inserts ~5 docs into db.saved_quotes under demo user (clients: Melinda Rankine, TEST_AAA, TEST_BBB, TEST_MULTI_A_*, TEST_MULTI_B_*) + 1 under fresh qa_* user; updates one qa_* user's business_name/abn/hourly_rate via PUT /api/auth/profile. Playwright run inserts ~3 more docs under demo (Test Quote A, Multi One, Multi Two) and sets demo's business_name='TEST_Acme Earthworks', abn='11122233344'. Safe to cleanup by client LIKE 'TEST_%' or business_name='TEST_Acme Earthworks'.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://pro-quote-builder-1.preview.emergentagent.com pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v. All 19 tests should pass. Frontend testids verified: wiz-qty-<key>, wiz-rate-<key>, wiz-linetotal-<key>, wiz-markup-input, wiz-markup-slider, wiz-live-totals, total-subtotal, total-markup, total-gst, total-final, wiz-save-quote (transitions to 'Draft Saved'), wiz-generate-final, wiz-reset, quote-row-0, quote-card-0, quote-detail-modal, quote-detail-export, quote-detail-close, business-meta-edit-btn, business-name-input, business-abn-input, business-meta-save. NOTE: quote-row-0 click in Playwright requires page.evaluate("document.querySelector('[data-testid=quote-row-0]').click()") - regular page.click(force=True) is intercepted. AI generation latency ~20-30s; allow 90s wait for wiz-live-totals after wiz-next on Step 2.
rca of the issue: n/a - all core flows green. Three minor UX notes (wiz-reset doesn't clear Step1 fields, dashboard sample-row fallback still wired, quote-row-0 click target needs better hit area) are tracked under action_items for main agent.
